SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #3 

The “And Justice for All” 



Ry Schwartz & Copy Hackers

2020



SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #2

About The “Simon Cowell Qualifier”

The Persuasion Deep Dive

Where to send it:

Themeplate

See it in action:

Example: Copy Hackers - 10x Freelance Copywriter (October 2018)



About The “And Justice For All” 



The “And Justice For All” is an advanced themeplate that works really well as a follow up to the “Simon Cowell Qualifier” covered in Session #2. 



In fact, they were stacked together as webinar invites for the 10x Freelance Copywriter 2017 Campaign, and essentially hit the same points using different persuasion hooks. 



Whereas the SCQ is about calling your prospect to a higher standard (aspiration) based on elements of EE (exclusive empowerment), the “And Justice for All” is all about making your prospect hyper-aware of the injustice between where they are -- and where they should be (based on those same EE elements).



Agitation occurs in the gap between how things are -- and how things should be. 



There’s a massive distinction between “should” and “could”. 



“Could” is what you daydream about in your passion planner with a chai latte and Bon Iver playing through your headphones. 



It’s aspiration. It’s not urgent. It has the energy of “that would be awesome, but it’s cool if I don’t get there fast… or get there at all”.



“Should” is what keeps you up at night. It’s what ignites a guttural clench of “not OKness” -- it’s the raw, unrefined form of FOMO that motivates action. 



FOMO is always rooted in some form of injustice. 



I SHOULD be there. I SHOULD be doing that. I SHOULD be at that level.



“Why have others with less talent, less experience, a smaller list, etc. been able to do that, and I’m still hanging out here in the land of mediocrity?”



If calling them to a higher standard in the SCQ wasn’t enough to move them to action -- the and “And Justice for All” should be able to create enough friction between where they are and where they should be to spark their motivation. 



There’s so much emotional juice in fixing or correcting an injustice and restoring a reality that SHOULD be given their actions, accomplishments, and ambitions (all forms of EE). 



Moving Down Maslow’s Pyramid



Move the outcomes that your product delivers down from the aspirational to the level of safety (where feeling emotionally unsettled by injustice resides). 



When you make your outcomes aspirational -- they’re gonna wait to do that till they have their shit figured out to pursue it…



And newsflash: 



When was the last time you felt like you have your shit figured out?



Aspirational is in the “someday”



It brings inertia. Restricts movement. 



Our body doesn’t spring into fight or flight for aspiration



Stop treating desire as something separate from pain. Start treating desire is the removal of an injustice.



Pleasure is the moment immediately after it.

The Persuasion Deep Dive



Persuasion Principle #1: Injustice (as the MOHT): 



The signaling of injustice and the gap between how things are -- and how things should be is often stronger than “pain” alone. 



Persuasion Principle #2: Redemption (as the MOHP): 



The removal of an injustice (and restoration of how things should be) is often more powerful and emotionally compelling than a desired outcome or aspiration on its own.  



Persuasion Principle #3: The STRONG need to close the gap:



We Invest an irrational amount of resources (time/money/energy/attention/emotions) to close the gap between Injustice and Redemption



You want to break through inertia? 



Give your prospect the chance to undo an implied injustice and restore homeostasis. Don’t give them a chance to explore what COULD be -- give them a chance to restore what SHOULD be.





How this links up with PASO



Pain (as the existence of injustice)

Agitation (in the friction between where they are and where they should be) 

Solution (as the removal of the injustice -- and restoring what should be) 

Opportunity (as the plastic spoon to get there)





Where to send it:

As an invitation to a conversion event (ie. webinar invite). Ideally as a follow up to the Simon Cowell Qualifier

As a standalone sales email

Message Mining Q’s:

For EE: 



What has my ideal client already (and recently) achieved? (EE1 - Achievement)

What actions are my ideal client currently taking that should set them up for a better outcome than they’re currently experiencing (EE2 - Actions)

What higher aspirations/expectations does my ideal client have for themselves (EE3 - Aspiration) 



Themeplate

[The Existential MOHT]

[Agitate with a stack of EE (action, accomplishment, ambition) -- past - present - future explaining why that MOHT shouldn’t be happening for them] 

[State the Injustice with a “should”] 

[Diagnose the injustice]

[CTA to help them remove the injustice] aka give them the spoon. 

[Fascinations to support the CTA] 

[Final CTA]

See it in action: 

10x Freelance Copywriter 2017



Body:



A modest 45K/year.

Nights, weekends and holidays super-glued to a Starbucks table. 

(the one next to a wall plug because your 2014 Macbook Air has the lifespan of a diabetic fruitfly) 

Constantly keeping an eye out for local job openings -- 'cos even though you hate to admit it, at this point, you might just be willing to trade in your precious freedom for a fixed salary, 401K and company-sponsored trip to the dentist. All your friends seem to love it. Well, love is a strong word. They tolerate it. Okay they hate their jobs. But they get every weekend off! That means they're happy 28.57% of the time. So. 

This is it.

Or is it?

The life of the “average” freelance copywriter.

The most frustrating part is…

… you have the talent.

… you continue to sharpen it through books, courses and training.

... you've seen what average looks like, and you're light years ahead of it.

… and in a perfect world, that *should* be enough to grow your freelance income to, say, DOUBLE what that stingy hiring manager is trying to strangle you into since he knows he has all the leverage.

Only problem is this:

You keep making the same old mistakes.

And those mistakes keep you stuck in freelancing purgatory.

So lemme help you. Start by understanding this: 

Being a freelance copywriter IS a super valuable, six-figure-a-year gig. It’s in its DNA.

But WHY isn't your freelance biz where it should be??

I may have the answer you seek. I've seen 100s of freelance copywriters stunt their growth with *cringe-worthy* moves... like sending their proposals via email… and in the rare case they actually land a call with an A-list client, they handle said call with the same composure as a lifelong Trekkie next in line to meet Shatner at Comic-con.

Stop it. Plz. 

For the love of, Picard… get your sh*t together.



At 9am PST / noon EST TODAY, I’m going live to spare you from 4 cringe-worthy mistakes.

The very mistakes that are chopping oh-so-many freelance copywriting careers off at the knees. (The blood! The horror!) 

I've started calling it a "fix-it shop" in my head, as I've put this training together. Cuz we're fixing real problems. In a very short period of time.

Go here to join me and a few hundred other freelance copywriters for get clear, no-BS answers re:

Why so few prospects bother responding to your proposals

The competitive advantage you're soooo close to having in your grasp

How the world's best-paid copywriters attract incredible clients

The 1 thing that's separating you from the promise of an easy freelancing life

Why you've been spinning your wheels - and how to get a grip on earning well

Fix even one of these mistakes, and 2018 could be the year you've been dying to have.

See you in a few!

~jo

PS: Try to join 15 mins early for our pre-show. We've got a special guest coming in!
